description: Evidence field vocabulary for the Lumina algebra-intro module (gr 8). Extends the pre-algebra base with four deterministic diagnostic signals for Laws 3 and 5. All fields produced by the deterministic algebra parser tool — never by LLM estimation.

response_latency_sec.description: Wall-clock seconds from prompt delivery to response receipt. Universal base field.

off_task_ratio.description: Fraction of the response that does not engage with the current task. 0.0 = fully on-task, 1.0 = fully off-task. Universal base field.

correctness.description: Whether the subject's answer is correct, incorrect, or partially correct. Null when correctness cannot be determined.

hint_used.description: True if the subject's message explicitly requested or acknowledged a hint.

frustration_marker_count.description: Count of frustration signals detected in the subject's message.

repeated_error.description: True if the same error type was made in consecutive turns.

step_count.description: Number of distinct algebraic transformation steps shown in the subject's response.

min_steps.description: Minimum number of steps required for a correct solution to this task (from curriculum).

solution_value.description: The numeric or symbolic value extracted from the subject's proposed solution.

equivalence_preserved.description: True if each transformation step preserves algebraic equivalence. Law 1 (Balance Principle).

reversibility_order_correct.description: True if constant was removed before coefficient was divided. Null for single-step equations. Law 2 (Inverse Operations).

inequality_direction_correct.description: True if inequality symbol was correctly flipped when dividing/multiplying by a negative. Null when no negative divisor present. Law 2 (Inverse Operations — inequality variant).

substitution_valid.description: True if the student's proposed solution (x, y) satisfies ALL equations in the system when substituted. False if any equation is violated. Null on parse error or missing system context. Deterministic: set by check_system_verification tool call. Law 3 (Interchangeability).

relationship_correctly_mapped.description: True if the slope or rate-of-change value extracted from the student's work matches the correct value from the curriculum task context. False if the slope is wrong (e.g., inverted). Null when no slope value is detectable in the student's work. Deterministic: set by check_slope_computation tool call. Law 5 (Relational Mapping).
